title: Prose Heavy Surface Inventory
goal: structured-first-operating-updates first child; inventory recurring prose-writing surfaces before replacing, constraining, generating, structuring, shrinking, or removing them.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q
uv run ruff check src tests scripts
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section prose_surface_inventory --format json
uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Prose-heavy surfaces are inventoried with owner and purpose.
Each recurring prose pattern has a target status: keep, constrain, generate, structure, shrink, remove, or archive-only.
Top follow-up candidates are routed to implementation issues.
Inventory links to structured-file inventory where relevant.
The inventory is discoverable without broad file search.

execution_run.executor: Codex with Curie and Hilbert read-only subagent review
execution_run.what happened: Created a schema-backed prose surface inventory, added a validated contract loader, registered the contract in structured-file, contract-consumption, owner, and report contracts, and exposed a compact report section for follow-up routing.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace contracts/schemas, contract tooling loader, report payload/section hints, focused tests, and planning state.
execution_run.changed surfaces: prose_surface_inventory contract/schema, structured_file_inventory, python_contract_consumption, contract_inventory, report_contract, workspace_report schema, cli report payload, reporting_support, tests, and planning state.
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run ruff check src tests scripts; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section prose_surface_inventory --format json; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed ... --format json; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py --strict; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json.
execution_run.result for continuation: Continue #530 with #532 closeout/archive prose generation first, then #534 and #535.

proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Focused contract/workspace tests, contract tooling, structured-file inventory, ruff, report section output, root CLI authority, planning strict check, planning doctor, and proof selector passed.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: structured-first-operating-updates first child; inventory recurring prose-writing surfaces before replacing, constraining, generating, structuring, shrinking, or removing them.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The prose_surface_inventory contract classifies eight operating surface families by owner, purpose, classification, target status, recurring patterns, structured owner, prose role, validation refs, and follow-up issues; report --section prose_surface_inventory exposes the map.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: schema-backed prose-heavy operating surface inventory with compact report discovery

durable_residue.learned constraint: Recurring operating prose should be classified by target status and routed to structured owners before adding templates or generators.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: The system feels smaller when agents can query prose burden and follow-up routing instead of rereading archives, templates, Memory notes, and review docs.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: when recurring prose-authoring surfaces, classifications, or structured-first follow-up issues change

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #531 inventories and routes the prose burden, while #530 still needs #532, #534, and #535 implementation slices.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: prose_surface_inventory contract, report section output, contract tooling tests, structured-file inventory entry, and follow-up issues #532, #534, and #535 under parent #530
closure_check.reopen trigger: a recurring prose-heavy operating surface lacks classification, target status, owner, or follow-up route

signals found: Subagents accelerated inventory verification and found missing registration/report-discovery hardening.
Proof selector surfaced direct CLI authority review for the report section payload.
The inventory itself should be discoverable through an existing report section rather than another startup doc.
signals fixed: Registered the inventory as schema-backed, contract-consumed, owner-classified, and report-discoverable.
Used existing report section selector instead of adding a new command.
signals routed: #534 for constrained review, handoff, and self-improvement templates.
#526/#528 continue to own helper/scaffold and validation-friction routing patterns.
